homepage.hero.subtitle.message: Trusted Open Source Supply Chain Management

homepage.hero.description.message:
ISO/IEC 5230 & 18974 Based
Enterprise Open Source Management System Guide

homepage.trustbar.thesis.message: Trust open source — without outsourcing that trust to a vendor.

homepage.trustbar.pillar3.desc.message: CC BY 4.0 · no vendor consulting required

homepage.cta.intro.title.message: From standards to pipeline, all in one place

homepage.cta.feature1.desc.message: Build an enterprise open source management system from scratch to completion, based on ISO/IEC 5230 & 18974.

homepage.cta.feature2.desc.message: Integrate security into your development pipeline. Covers SAST, SCA, container security, and CI/CD automation.

homepage.cta.feature3.desc.message: Manage AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot alongside open source compliance.

homepage.cta.section.subtitle.message: Complete your ISO/IEC 5230 & 18974 self-certification with this free guide from the OpenChain KWG community.

homepage.hero.subtitle.portal.message: Trust open source — without outsourcing that trust to a vendor. Build an ISO/IEC 5230 & 18974 open source management system yourself, with no vendor consulting, all the way to a self-certification declaration — guided step by step, even from zero.

homepage.hero.stat1.label.message: ISO/IEC standards for license compliance and security assurance self-certification

homepage.hero.stat2.label.message: Agent chain that generates artifacts from org design to self-certification

homepage.features.subtitle.message: A standards-based management system, a security pipeline, and AI coding compliance — follow the path that fits your role.

homepage.showcase.subtitle.message: Preview the artifacts auto-generated for your company through these best-practice samples.

homepage.showcase.policy.caption.message: Policy & org — license approval tiers and the RACI responsibility matrix

homepage.showcase.sbom.caption.message: SBOM & vulnerabilities — CycloneDX analysis and triage reports

homepage.showcase.conformance.caption.message: Self-certification — conformance checklist and declaration draft

homepage.whykwg.subtitle.message: Not competition, but completion. KWG points the way with standards and blank templates; TrustedOSS turns that into executable artifacts with AI and automation.

homepage.whykwg.kwg.desc.message: Tells you what the standard requires and why. Provides the international standards, an enterprise OSS management guide, and blank templates.

homepage.whykwg.toss.desc.message: Helps you actually achieve the standard with AI and automation, on a single path from zero to self-certification.

homepage.whykwg.toss.point2.message: Copy-paste CI workflows and Rules, no-API-key demos

homepage.whykwg.toss.point3.message: Extends to DevSecOps and AI coding governance

homepage.whykwg.note.message: TrustedOSS synchronizes KWG content and attributes it under CC BY 4.0.

homepage.hero.eyebrow.message: Free self-study guide by OpenChain KWG

homepage.finalcta.subtitle.message: A free OpenChain KWG guide, all the way to self-certification. No install, no cost, no vendor lock-in.

homepage.hero.subtitle.content.message: Build an ISO/IEC 5230 & 18974 open source management system step by step, with no vendor consulting — all the way to a self-certification declaration. No prior experience needed.

homepage.roadmap.title.message: From zero to self-certification, step by step

homepage.roadmap.subtitle.message: Just follow the order. An agent at each step generates company-specific artifacts for you.

homepage.roadmap.s0.desc.message: Grasp the big picture of supply chain security and the two standards.

homepage.roadmap.s1.desc.message: Prepare the foundation for self-study and artifact generation.

homepage.roadmap.s2.desc.message: Define governance owners with a responsibility (RACI) model.

homepage.roadmap.s3.desc.message: Establish license approval tiers and policy documents.

homepage.roadmap.s4.desc.message: Design operating procedures: review, approval, disclosure.

homepage.roadmap.s5.desc.message: Generate and analyze SBOMs and manage vulnerabilities with tools.

homepage.roadmap.s6.desc.message: Build the capability to keep the system running over time.

homepage.roadmap.s7.desc.message: Check conformance and complete a self-certification declaration.

homepage.meta.title.message: Trusted OSS · Trusted Open Source Supply Chain Management

homepage.meta.description.message: A practical guide to building an enterprise open source management system based on ISO/IEC 5230 & 18974

journey.note.message: Checkmarks are stored only in this browser. Mark each step as you finish it to track your progress.

glossary.sbom.message: Software Bill of Materials — a list of every open source component in a product, with versions and licenses.

glossary.spdx.message: A standard format for SBOMs and licensing data (Linux Foundation).

glossary.cyclonedx.message: One of the standard SBOM formats (OWASP).

glossary.copyleft.message: Copyleft — a license family that requires derivative works to be released under the same license (GPL, AGPL, etc.).

glossary.permissive.message: Permissive license — few obligations beyond attribution (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD).

glossary.cve.message: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures — the unique identifier assigned to a known vulnerability.

glossary.cvss.message: Vulnerability severity score — risk expressed on a 0-10 scale.

glossary.vex.message: Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange — states whether a vulnerability actually affects your product.

glossary.sca.message: Software Composition Analysis — checks open source components for vulnerabilities and license issues.

glossary.openchain.message: The international open source compliance standards project (ISO/IEC 5230 and 18974).

glossary.self-certification.message: Declaring on your own, without an external audit, that you meet the OpenChain requirements.
